column1: # Gate-runner rows — THE injection engine's config (one runner consumes this table). TAB-separated.
# columns: name  event  matcher(tool regex; "." = any/none)  field(jq expr → query text)  threshold  mode  inject
# mode: shadow (score→log only) | live (score→log→inject) | off. INJECTION FLIP = set rows 1-2 to `live`
#   (after LOOP_ENABLED=1 re-enables the loop). All start shadow so re-enabling the loop injects nothing.
# inject: context = hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (→ the CALLER's conversation; correct for prompt-submit/
#   friction/seeking/hazard). prompt = hookSpecificOutput.updatedInput with tool_input.prompt amended (→ the
#   SUBAGENT's own prompt; required for subagent-spawn — additionalContext on a Task hook lands in the PARENT,
#   empirically verified). updatedInput is wholesale-replace, so the runner rebuilds the full tool_input.
# threshold gates the inject stage only, PER CANDIDATE (each injected slug needs its OWN score ≥ threshold, so a
#   deduped high-scorer can't carry sub-threshold tail candidates in); shadow rows log regardless of threshold.
# rows 1-2 threshold 5.1 = the A/B precision-sweep max-recall operating point (recall 24/24, false-inject 12/15;
#   strictly dominates 0 — same recall, fewer false-injects; recall-favoring by operator policy, documented in the threshold decision record). SNAPSHOT calibration:
#   BM25 scores are non-stationary (IDF/N/avgdl shift as the corpus grows), so probes_ci_test WARNs when the
#   sweep's max-recall T diverges >1.0 from this value. On first drift, evolve this constant into a QUANTILE rule
#   (T = pXX of the negative-set top-1 scores, recomputed per rev by probes-CI) — self-calibrating; triggered then.
# PHASE 3 — rows 3-5 are NOT registered at landing (merge_hooks wires only the 1/turn gates 1-2 + PreCompact),
#   so they neither score nor add latency until Phase 3 explicitly registers them. Registration ORDER when it does:
#   1) friction FIRST — PostToolUse, pays python only on an error-bearing result (near-zero cost, loosest/highest-value).
#   2) seeking + hazard TOGETHER, and only alongside the per-turn injection cap (≤2/turn) — they are synchronous
#      PreToolUse (python before EVERY Grep/WebSearch/Bash/Edit), which is exactly what the cap + tuned thresholds justify.
#   Also pending: per-machine gate overrides via config.local.sh (spec'd, not yet implemented).
prompt-submit
subagent-spawn
friction
seeking
hazard
